message:
fix(mcp_client): redact secrets in mapped remote error text (ft-qde8p)

`map_mcp_error` copied `FrameworkMcpError.message` verbatim into
`McpClientError.message`, which the `list_tools` / `call_tool`
failure paths emit as `message = %mapped.message` at tracing::warn.
A remote server that echoed operator-supplied credentials in its
error payload would land the raw secret in diagnostic logs despite
the repo already having a `policy::Redactor` with matchers for
Anthropic/OpenAI/GitHub/AWS/Bearer/Stripe/DB-URL/OAuth patterns.

Wrap `err.message` through `Redactor::new().redact(...)` before
constructing the carried error message. The classification heuristic
(`to_ascii_lowercase().contains("timed out")` / "failed to spawn"
/ "spawn subprocess") still probes the raw message so structural
sentinels survive any secret-pattern match, but only the redacted
base flows into `McpClientError.message` (and therefore the warn
log). Server framing and non-secret context are preserved so
operator diagnostics stay readable.

Pinned by new unit test `map_mcp_error_redacts_secrets_in_remote_error_ft_qde8p`:
feeds a `sk-ant-…` shaped token into the remote error body and
asserts the mapped message carries `[REDACTED]`, drops the raw
secret, and retains the `server 'mock'` / `(401)` framing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
